title: President Trump Wants to Reopen Alcatraz Prison
summary: President Donald Trump is asking for $152 million to reopen Alcatraz, a famous old prison near San Francisco. Alcatraz closed in 1963 and is now a popular tourist spot run by the National Park Service, making money from visitors. Trump wants to turn it back into a modern, secure prison for very dangerous criminals. Some politicians, like Nancy Pelosi, think this is a bad idea because it would cost too much money and lose the tourism income. They say it's hard to run a prison on an island without running water, and all supplies must come by boat. Congress needs to approve the money for this plan to happen.

questions.question: Who needs to approve the money for reopening Alcatraz?

background_read: Alcatraz is an island prison near San Francisco that closed in 1963 because it was too expensive to run. Now, it's a popular place for tourists to visit, managed by the National Park Service, and it earns money from visitors. President Trump has a plan to turn it back into a prison for dangerous criminals, but some people disagree because it might waste money and stop people from visiting.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and politicians like Nancy Pelosi
WHAT: A plan to spend $152 million to reopen Alcatraz as a modern prison
WHERE: Alcatraz island near San Francisco, California
WHY: To house dangerous criminals, but critics say it's too costly and would lose tourism revenue

perspectives.description: Trump wants a secure prison for violent offenders and to invest in the Bureau of Prisons.
Politicians like Pelosi argue it wastes money, loses tourism income, and is impractical due to island challenges.
